_meta.purpose: PRE-REGISTERED physics predictions for the x0 LOAD slim-10. Written BEFORE extracting any fitted coefficient. The check script (physics_check.py) READS this file; it never writes it. Predictions are derived from rigid-body mechanics ALONE, independent of fitted values.
_meta.honesty_note: I (the author) have prior exposure to this dataset from earlier sessions. This is therefore NOT a blind pre-registration. The value is methodological: every sign / zero-structure / ordering prediction below follows from the EOM of a planar serial chain, NOT from inspecting coefficients. Several predictions (e.g. sin_q2 must be ~0 on J3/J4; cos_c4 same sign on all 3 joints; on-J2 gravity ordering sin_q2>cos_c3>cos_c4) were never checked in prior work.
_meta.robot: SO-ARM101, planar-dominant serial chain. Modelled joints J2=shoulder_lift, J3=elbow_flex, J4=wrist_flex. Identical high-gear-ratio STS3215 servos.
_meta.constraint: load/current channels are UNCALIBRATED. Absolute N*m magnitude checks are impossible. We therefore test only (a) SIGN, (b) zero/non-zero STRUCTURE across joints, (c) cross-joint and within-joint RELATIVE ORDERING. The STS3215 load register uses the same encoding on every (identical) servo, so raw coefficients are treated as roughly comparable across joints, with that caveat flagged.

eom.model: tau_j = sum_k M_jk(q) qddot_k + C_j(q,qdot) + g_j(q) + f_j(qdot, load)
eom.gravity: For a planar serial chain with absolute link angles theta_i = q2+...+qi, g_j(q) = sum_{i>=j} A_i cos(theta_i), with A_i = g * a_i * (sum_{k>=i} m_k). KEY STRUCTURE: A_i depends only on i, NOT on j -> the SAME absolute-angle term has the SAME coefficient on every joint j<=i, and is identically ABSENT for j>i (lower-triangular gravity coupling).
eom.encoder_phase: theta_2 -> column sin_q2, theta_3=c3 -> column cos_c3, theta_4=c4 -> column cos_c4. sin vs cos is fixed by each joint's encoder zero offset from horizontal; physically each is a sinusoid of one absolute link angle.
eom.inertia: Diagonal M_jj qddot_j dominates. At high gear ratio reflected motor inertia N^2*J_motor (identical across the 3 identical servos) dominates the diagonal; link inertia adds on top and is larger for more proximal joints.
eom.friction: f = b*qdot (viscous, column vL) + fc*sign(qdot) (Coulomb, smoothed as tanh(v/fc); columns tanh_vP and the velocity part of coul_sin_c3). Load-dependent Coulomb: normal/tooth force in the gearbox tracks gravity torque -> coul_sin_c3 = tanh(vL/fc)*sin(c3).
eom.not_in_rigid_eom: Transport delay / gear-train compliance (lag_vL_a, lag_aL_a) and band-pass acceleration (adiff) are NOT terms of the rigid-body EOM. They are drivetrain-dynamics / numerical-band constructs.

pred.reason_zero: q2 enters J3/J4 gravity ONLY through c3,c4 (cos_c3,cos_c4). The standalone i=2 term appears only for j=2 (lower-triangular). A large coefficient on J3 or J4 means this column is absorbing non-gravity (posture-friction / calibration), not link-2 gravity.
pred.sign: consistent on J2 (single dominant gravity term); polarity itself is set by unknown load sign, so we test internal consistency, not absolute sign

sin_q2.falsification: If |contrib| on J3 or J4 is comparable to its J2 value -> NOT link-2 gravity; reclassify empirical.

pred.reason_zero: i=3 term appears for j<=3 only; j=4 gravity has no theta_3 dependence.
pred.sign: SAME sign on J2 and J3 (j-independent A_3).

cos_c3.falsification: Opposite signs on J2 vs J3, OR a large coefficient on J4 -> term is not clean i=3 gravity.

pred.sign: SAME sign on ALL of J2,J3,J4 (j-independent A_4).

cos_c4.falsification: Any sign disagreement across the three joints -> not clean i=4 gravity.

pred.sign: SAME sign on all three joints (load responds to own acceleration with one polarity).
pred.cross_joint_order: |b_raw(J2)| >= |b_raw(J3)| >= |b_raw(J4)|  (reflected inertia: more mass beyond a more proximal joint). WEAK: identical servos share N^2*J_motor so values may be close.

aL.falsification: Sign differs across joints, OR J4 inertia coefficient strongly exceeds J2 -> not a clean inertia term.

pred.sign: SAME sign on all joints; AND consistent with the Coulomb terms (tanh_vP, coul_sin_c3 velocity part). All friction is dissipative -> one common velocity->load polarity.

vL.falsification: vL and tanh_vP opposite signs, or vL sign flips across joints -> not a clean friction term.

tanh_vP.eom_term: Coulomb friction fc*sign(qdot), smoothed on low-pass velocity vP

pred.sign: SAME sign as vL on every joint (both dissipative friction).
pred.note: use of the LONG low-pass band vP (vs raw v) is the empirical/drivetrain-memory part; the sign prediction is the physics part.

tanh_vP.falsification: Opposite sign to vL on a joint -> the column is not acting as Coulomb friction there.

coul_sin_c3.eom_term: load-dependent Coulomb: gearbox normal force ~ gravity torque -> tanh(vL/fc)*sin(c3)

pred.sign: no clean joint-independent sign prediction (product of friction sign and a gravity projection that changes sign with posture). Tested only for being non-negligible and for not contradicting the friction family.
pred.note: Mechanism (normal-force-dependent friction) is physical but the specific sin(c3) projection is partly empirical -> graded physics_weak.

coul_sin_c3.falsification: Negligible contribution on all joints -> the load-modulation hypothesis is unsupported.

lag_vL_a.eom_term: NONE (rigid-body EOM has no transport delay)

pred.mechanism: claimed: group delay through a compliant high-ratio gear train. NOT derivable from rigid-body EOM -> no sign/structure prediction is registered.

lag_vL_a.falsification: n/a -- there is no prior physical prediction to falsify; that is exactly why it is graded empirical.

lag_aL_a.eom_term: NONE (rigid-body EOM has no transport delay)

pred.mechanism: claimed: same drivetrain delay on the inertia channel. Not an EOM term.

adiff.eom_term: NONE (band-pass accel aS-aL; numerical/compliance band, not rigid-body)

pred.mechanism: claimed: fast drivetrain-compliance accel mode above the smoothed-inertia band. Not an EOM term.
